mechanism_narrative: An always-on rise in FEDFUNDS over the prior 3 months raises equity discount rates and reduces the present value of future S&P 500 cash flows, producing negative 20-day forward returns when the policy-rate trend is upward.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with policy rate above 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month changes in FEDFUNDS are not followed by lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than negative 3-month changes in FEDFUNDS in at least 60% of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE is very low and FEDFUNDS is high, additional labor-market tightness increases the probability of persistent wage inflation and restrictive policy, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses below 3.6% while FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets and restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the triggered mean return is not below the unconditional mean at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: An always-on positive trend in RSAFS signals resilient consumer spending and stronger expected corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 3-month increases in RSAFS do not predict higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than 3-month decreases in RSAFS across at least 65% of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A downside break in INDPRO signals weakening real activity and near-term earnings risk for cyclical sectors, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days after the break is observed.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below its 3-month moving average after being above it in the prior month
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO downside-crossing signals are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 58% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls through a key level while FEDFUNDS is elevated, rate-sensitive housing demand is transmitting monetary restriction into real activity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below 1400 while FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if this joint trigger produces average 60-day S&P 500 returns that are not below the unconditional 60-day average, or if hit-rate for negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong PAYEMS growth combined with low UNRATE indicates durable household income and limited recession risk, supporting earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average monthly gain exceeds 150 thousand and UNRATE remains below 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have lower average 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered observations, or if the positive-return hit rate is below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: CPIAUCSL reacceleration while FEDFUNDS is already high signals that inflation pressure is forcing policy to remain restrictive, raising discount-rate risk and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change crosses above its 12-month change while FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint inflation-policy trigger is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if its mean return is not below zero at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A positive quarterly change in GDPC1 confirms broad real demand growth and reduces near-term recession risk, creating a positive S&P 500 response over the next 5 trading days after the GDP information is incorporated.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 crosses above its prior-quarter value
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 quarterly-change signals are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 50% of observations or have a mean return indistinguishable from zero at p > 0.10.
